threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think you can hop on the Tube without checking for strikes—good luck with that!
Last night on the 47, a guy started singing opera. By the time we hit London Bridge, half the bus was clapping. Only in Bermondsey, I tell you.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a kid playing in the street, dodging the coming tram while balancing an ice cream cone perfectly—only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Kreuzberg, it's normal to find people selling homemade vegan cakes at bus stops between the trains. Only here do baked goods become part of the public transport experience.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their ability to navigate BART delays while still managing to get a burrito from La Taqueria before it closes.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy serenading Muni riders with a ukulele by the 4th Street station. Some clapped, others just plugged in. That's SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just for nightlife, but many locals avoid the rambles of Travessera. The real charm is in the quiet plazas during the day.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell the newcomers; they still take photos of the ‘famous’ La Padrera as if it’s not just another building on Passeig de Gràcia.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still queue for a latte at the trendy café instead of grabbing a takeaway and finding a spot in the park. That's a rookie move.
Last night on the 133, a guy serenaded the bus with his guitar. By the time we hit Stockwell, half the passengers were singing along. Wild vibe.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day a kid asked me for change at the Kottbusser Tor, and I realized I wasn’t just passing through anymore.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you experience spontaneous techno parties in abandoned buildings—no schedule, just the bass echoing off the walls.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local when they know to avoid the BART at Civic Center during rush hour – those smoke evacuations really clear the crowds.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy performing Shakespeare on the 6 bus today, while the driver just shook his head. Muni magic is real, even if the buses aren’t.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia’s plazas are just for selfies, but locals use them for spontaneous petanques and late-night gatherings – it’s a vibe.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You’ll see them staring at the metro map, not realizing the L2 is the only line that runs on a Sunday. They still think Sundays are for trains.
